help.title: Help & Crisis Hotlines
help.subtitle: You are not alone. Find immediate support here.
help.meta_description: Free, anonymous LGBTQIA+ crisis hotlines and counselling services worldwide. You are not alone.
help.emergency_body: Call emergency services immediately: 112 (EU) or 911 (US/CA). Every second counts.
help.disclaimer: Queer Guide does not replace professional help.
help.no_results: No hotlines match these filters. Try "All countries" or check the international directories.
help.error_body: In acute danger, call your local emergency number: 112 (EU) or 911 (US/CA).
help.reveal_aria: Click anywhere to show the page again
help.hidden_hint: Page hidden. Click anywhere to show again.
help.expect_1: You'll hear a short greeting. You don't have to give your name.
help.expect_2: You can hang up at any time. You can call back as often as you need.
help.expect_3: Free hotlines in DE/AT/CH don't appear on itemized phone bills.
help.expect_4: If you can't speak, ask for text or chat — most hotlines offer it.
help.self_help_desc: Short techniques you can use while you wait, or instead of a call if you're not ready.
help.breathing_1: Breathe in through your nose for 4 seconds.
help.breathing_3: Breathe out through your mouth for 8 seconds.
help.grounding_intro: Name, out loud or in your head:
help.safety_plan_desc: A short written plan you can pull up when things get bad — your warning signs, coping steps, people to call, and reasons to live.
help.report_aria: Report a problem with this hotline
help.report_desc: Thanks for helping keep this list accurate. Reports are reviewed by our team.

sos.confirmBody: This will immediately notify all {{count}} of your friends. Your current location will be shared if available.
sos.cooldown: Please wait before sending another alert
sos.notificationBody: {{name}} needs help! Please check on them.
sos.error: Failed to send alert. Please try again.

signInGate.title: Sign in to install the extension
signInGate.subtitle: The queer.guide capture extension lets signed-in members suggest venues, events, hotels and more from any webpage.

heading.title: queer.guide capture
heading.subtitle: Browser extension that turns any webpage into a structured suggestion for our moderators.

connect.title: Connect this browser
connect.body: Sign your queer.guide session into the extension so you can submit content from any webpage.
connect.signInPrompt: Sign in to your queer.guide account to connect this browser and start submitting.

done.title: Connected.
done.body: Open the extension popup on any page to capture content for queer.guide.

s1.title: Download the latest build
s1.body: We don't have the extension on the Chrome Web Store yet. For now, grab the latest signed build.
s1.note: Unzip it somewhere you won't accidentally delete (e.g. ~/Applications/queer-guide-extension).

s2.title: Load it in Chrome
s2.l1: Open chrome://extensions in a new tab.
s2.l3: Click Load unpacked and pick the unzipped folder.
s2.l4: The queer.guide puzzle icon appears in your toolbar — pin it for one-click access.
s2.note: Other Chromium browsers (Edge, Brave, Arc) work the same way. Firefox / Safari are not supported yet.

s3.title: Reload this page and Connect
s3.body: Once Chrome confirms the extension loaded, refresh this page. A “Connect” button will appear and one click signs the extension into your queer.guide account.

dev.label: Want to build it yourself or contribute?

smart.title: Smart capture
smart.body: Reads JSON-LD, OpenGraph, microdata and DOM heuristics — most pages just work.

privacy.title: Privacy-first
privacy.body: Only runs when you click. No host permissions, no background tracking.

review.title: Always reviewed
review.body: Submissions land in a moderation queue, never live until approved.
